topic.what: Cross-scene lip-motion visual speaker recognition using event cameras
topic.how: NeuroLip processes sparse event streams via TVE, SSE, and PCR to capture fine-grained motion patterns invariant to viewpoint and illumination changes
topic.why_matters: Enables practical biometric enrollment under a single controlled condition while maintaining robustness across unseen deployment environments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Identity recognition accuracy under cross-scene protocol (train on SI-0°, test on unseen conditions)
characteristics.context: Cross-scene protocol with train on single controlled condition

characteristics.what_it_measures: Identity recognition accuracy under matched-scene conditions
characteristics.vs_baseline: On par with best video-based methods

characteristics.what_it_measures: Few-shot domain adaptation accuracy with K shots from target condition
characteristics.vs_baseline: +12.31% absolute gain from zero-shot to 1-shot on SI-45°
characteristics.context: K-shot fine-tuning after cross-scene zero-shot pre-training

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recognition accuracy with varying training samples per subject
characteristics.evidence: Table VIII training sample size sweep
characteristics.context: Data efficiency evaluation under matched SI-0° condition

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ablation: impact of LTA, SSE, and PCR components
characteristics.value: 71.68% (Full) vs 69.22% (w/o LTA) vs 71.36% (w/o SSE&PCR) vs 69.44% (w/o PCR)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Each component contributes complementary cross-scene robustness
characteristics.context: Ablation on SI-0° training, evaluated on SI-45° and II-0°
Content sensitivity analysis under SI-0° matched condition

novelty: First cross-scene lip-motion visual speaker recognition framework
DVSpeaker event-based lip-motion dataset with 50 subjects across 4 scene conditions
Temporal-aware Voxel Encoding with learnable temporal allocation preserving sub-bin dynamics
Structure-aware Spatial Enhancer with direction-aware smoothing for vertical lip motion
Polarity Consistency Regularization preserving motion-direction cues from event polarities
Strict cross-scene evaluation protocol (train single scene, test unseen viewpoints and illumination)
open_problems: Extreme viewpoint generalization (SI-90°) remains challenging for all methods
Large-angle geometric distortion and self-occlusion disrupt lip motion patterns
Extension to unconstrained speaking content beyond digit articulation
Adaptive polarity representation for severe low-illumination noise scenarios
Viewpoint-invariant motion modeling for orthogonal camera angles
